title: Solid stores + Tauri bridge layer
analysis:
Task 85 creates the reactive state management layer and IPC bridge for the Reify GUI frontend. Task 81 (scaffold) is a dependency but the gui/ directory does not exist on any branch or worktree (checked main and task/82-85, all at same commit with no gui/), so this task must bootstrap the minimal SolidJS project scaffold as a prerequisite.

The IPC types to mirror are defined in the task description: MeshData, ValueData, ConstraintData, SourceLocation, FileData, GuiState, plus EvaluationStatus. The Rust-side types in reify-types (Value enum in value.rs, Mesh struct in geometry.rs with vertices: Vec<f32>/indices: Vec<u32>/normals: Option<Vec<f32>>, Constraint types in constraint.rs, SourceSpan/Diagnostic in diagnostics.rs) inform the TypeScript shape but the IPC types are serialized/simplified versions.

The bridge layer wraps Tauri invoke() and listen() APIs with typed functions. Since Tauri won't be available in test environments, the bridge is mocked via vi.mock().

Three Solid stores (engine, selection, editor) use createStore from solid-js/store. Tests use Vitest with jsdom and SolidJS createRoot for reactive test isolation. The engine store subscribes to Tauri events via bridge, tested by mocking bridge event listeners.

Key design: Factory functions (createXStore) for test isolation. Record<string, T> for store collections (Solid proxies don't work with Map/Set). string[] for dirtyFiles (same reason).

--- POST-REVIEW ADDITIONS ---

Three blocking issues from code review:

1. [type_safety_violation] editorStore.ts:54 — setCursorPosition uses column! non-null assertion when column is optional. Fixed in steps 13-14 with column ?? 0.

2. [missing_batch_for_reactive_updates] engineStore.ts:54-64 — applyValueUpdates and applyConstraintUpdates call setState per-item in a loop without batch(). Each setState triggers a separate synchronous reactive flush. With bulk updates (100+ parameters from eval engine), this causes N re-render cycles instead of 1. Fix: wrap loops in batch() from solid-js. Steps 18-19.

3. [resource_leak] engineStore.ts:70-83 — subscribeToEvents() uses Promise.all(). If any listen() rejects, Promise.all rejects entirely and successfully-resolved UnlistenFn values are silently discarded — those event subscriptions leak for the lifetime of the webview. Fix: use Promise.allSettled, collect fulfilled UnlistenFn values, warn on rejections, return cleanup for successful listeners only. Steps 20-21.

Additional improvement: MeshData uses number[] instead of Float32Array/Uint32Array. Fixed in steps 15-16 with RawMeshData wire type and bridge conversion.

prerequisites.description: Create minimal gui/ project scaffold: package.json with solid-js, @tauri-apps/api (^2), vitest, @solidjs/testing-library, jsdom, vite, vite-plugin-solid, typescript. tsconfig.json with strict mode, JSX preserve for solid-js. vite.config.ts with solid plugin. vitest.config.ts with jsdom environment. index.html entry point. Minimal src/index.tsx and src/App.tsx. Run pnpm install. Add gui/node_modules to .gitignore.

steps.description: Create gui/src/__tests__/types.typecheck.ts: compile-time type check importing all types from types.ts. Creates typed variables asserting: MeshData with entityPath/vertices/indices/normals, ValueData with cellId/entityPath/memberName/value/determinacy, ConstraintData with constraintId/entityPath/satisfaction/messages, SourceLocation with filePath/line/column, FileData with path/content/language, GuiState with meshes/values/constraints/files/evalStatus, EvaluationStatus with phase union and optional progress, type aliases MeshUpdate/ValueUpdate/ConstraintUpdate. Won't compile since types.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/types.ts with all IPC types: MeshData (entityPath: string, vertices: Float32Array, indices: Uint32Array, normals: Float32Array | null), ValueData (cellId/entityPath/memberName/value/determinacy: string), ConstraintData (constraintId/entityPath: string, satisfaction: 'satisfied'|'violated'|'indeterminate', messages: string[]), SourceLocation (filePath: string, line/column: number), FileData (path/content/language: string), GuiState (meshes/values/constraints/files arrays + evalStatus), EvaluationStatus (phase: 'idle'|'evaluating'|'resolving', progress?: number), type aliases MeshUpdate/ValueUpdate/ConstraintUpdate. Run tsc --noEmit.

Create gui/src/__tests__/bridge.test.ts: mock @tauri-apps/api/core invoke() and @tauri-apps/api/event listen() via vi.mock(). Tests: (1) getInitialState calls invoke('get_initial_state'). (2) setParameter calls invoke('set_parameter', {cellId, value}). (3) updateSource calls invoke('update_source', {filePath, content}). (4) onMeshUpdate calls listen('mesh-update', cb) returns unlisten. (5) onEvaluationStatus calls listen('evaluation-status', cb). Fails because bridge.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/bridge.ts: 9 command functions (getInitialState, setParameter, updateSource, saveFile, openFile, exportGeometry, getSourceLocation, focusEntity, lspRequest) wrapping invoke(). 6 event listeners (onMeshUpdate, onValueUpdate, onConstraintUpdate, onEvaluationStatus, onDiagnostics, onFileChanged) wrapping listen() extracting event.payload. Returns Promise<UnlistenFn>. All bridge tests pass.

Create gui/src/__tests__/selectionStore.test.ts in createRoot: (1) initial null selectedEntity/hoveredEntity. (2) selectEntity('Bracket') sets selectedEntity. (3) selectEntity(null) clears. (4) hoverEntity sets/clears. (5) select/hover independent. Fails because selectionStore.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/stores/selectionStore.ts: createSelectionStore() factory returning { state, selectEntity, hoverEntity }. State: { selectedEntity: string | null, hoveredEntity: string | null }. Uses Solid createStore. All selection tests pass.

Create gui/src/__tests__/editorStore.test.ts in createRoot: (1) initial empty state. (2) openFile adds FileData, sets activeFile. (3) duplicate open doesn't add twice. (4) closeFile removes, handles activeFile switch. (5) closeFile removes from dirtyFiles. (6) markDirty/markClean toggles. (7) setActiveFile. (8) setCursorPosition. Fails because editorStore.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/stores/editorStore.ts: createEditorStore() factory returning { state, openFile, closeFile, setActiveFile, markDirty, markClean, setCursorPosition }. State: { openFiles: FileData[], activeFile: string | null, dirtyFiles: string[], cursorPosition: {line: number, column: number} | null }. All editor tests pass.

Create gui/src/__tests__/engineStore.test.ts in createRoot: (1) initial empty state, evalStatus {phase:'idle'}. (2) initFromState populates from GuiState. (3) applyMeshUpdate upserts by entityPath. (4) applyValueUpdates batch-upserts by cellId. (5) applyConstraintUpdates batch-upserts. (6) setEvalStatus updates. (7) subscribeToEvents wires bridge listeners, returns cleanup. Fails because engineStore.ts doesn't exist.

Create gui/src/stores/engineStore.ts: createEngineStore() factory returning { state, initFromState, applyMeshUpdate, applyValueUpdates, applyConstraintUpdates, setEvalStatus, subscribeToEvents }. State: { meshes: Record<string, MeshData>, values: Record<string, ValueData>, constraints: Record<string, ConstraintData>, evalStatus: EvaluationStatus }. subscribeToEvents wires bridge listeners, returns cleanup fn. All engine tests pass.
Run full test suite (vitest run) and type-check (tsc --noEmit). Verify all tests pass and TypeScript compiles cleanly.
Create gui/src/stores/index.ts barrel re-exporting all three store factories. Final cleanup and verification.

REVIEW FIX: Add failing test to editorStore.test.ts for setCursorPosition called with line only (no column argument). Test: setCursorPosition(10) should produce { line: 10, column: 0 } — verifying the column defaults to 0 rather than being undefined. This test will fail because the current implementation uses column! which passes undefined through.

REVIEW FIX: Fix setCursorPosition in editorStore.ts to use `column ?? 0` instead of `column!`. This ensures that calling setCursorPosition(line) without a column argument safely defaults to column 0, matching the declared type { line: number; column: number }. The editorStore tests (including the new step-13 test) pass.

REVIEW FIX: Add failing test to bridge.test.ts verifying that onMeshUpdate converts wire-format number[] arrays to typed arrays. Test: simulate Tauri delivering { entity_path, vertices: [0,1,2], indices: [0,1,2], normals: [0,0,1] } as the event payload, then assert the callback receives vertices as Float32Array, indices as Uint32Array, and normals as Float32Array. The test will fail because bridge.ts currently passes through raw JSON payloads without conversion.

REVIEW FIX: Change MeshData in types.ts to use typed arrays (vertices: Float32Array, indices: Uint32Array, normals: Float32Array | null). Add RawMeshData interface with number[] fields representing the JSON wire format from Tauri IPC. Add convertRawMesh(raw: RawMeshData): MeshData function that calls new Float32Array(raw.vertices), new Uint32Array(raw.indices), and conditionally new Float32Array(raw.normals). Update bridge.ts: onMeshUpdate listens for RawMeshData, converts via convertRawMesh before invoking callback. Similarly update getInitialState to convert GuiState.meshes from raw to typed. Update types.typecheck.ts to use Float32Array/Uint32Array constructors in sample data. Update engineStore.test.ts sampleMesh to use typed arrays. Update bridge.test.ts existing expectations where MeshData appears. All tests pass and tsc --noEmit is clean.

REVIEW FIX VERIFICATION: Run full test suite (vitest run) and type-check (tsc --noEmit). Verify all 4 test files pass and TypeScript compiles with no errors. Confirm MeshData uses typed arrays throughout and setCursorPosition safely defaults column.

REVIEW FIX [missing_batch_for_reactive_updates]: Add failing tests to engineStore.test.ts verifying that applyValueUpdates and applyConstraintUpdates each trigger exactly 1 reactive update cycle when called with multiple items, not N. Test approach: inside createRoot, create the engine store, then use createEffect from solid-js to track reactive notifications — the effect reads JSON.stringify(state.values) and increments an update counter (initialized to -1 to account for the initial effect run). Call applyValueUpdates with 3 ValueData items. Assert the counter equals 1 (one batched notification), not 3 (three separate flushes). Add a second test with the same pattern for applyConstraintUpdates: effect reads JSON.stringify(state.constraints), call with 3 ConstraintData items, assert counter equals 1. These tests FAIL against current code because each setState('values', key, val) in the unbatched loop triggers a separate synchronous reactive flush in SolidJS.

REVIEW FIX [missing_batch_for_reactive_updates]: In engineStore.ts, import { batch } from 'solid-js'. Wrap the loop in applyValueUpdates: batch(() => { for (const v of updates) setState('values', v.cell_id, v); }). Wrap the loop in applyConstraintUpdates: batch(() => { for (const c of updates) setState('constraints', c.node_id, c); }). This coalesces all per-item setState calls into a single reactive notification, so downstream effects/computations re-run once per bulk update rather than once per item. All engine store tests pass including the new batch verification tests from step-18.

REVIEW FIX [resource_leak]: Add failing test to engineStore.test.ts verifying subscribeToEvents handles partial listener failures without leaking resources. Test: mock onConstraintUpdate to reject with Error('event not available'), while onMeshUpdate, onValueUpdate, and onEvaluationStatus resolve with unlisten fns (vi.fn()). Call subscribeToEvents(). Assert: (1) it resolves (does not reject/throw) and returns a cleanup function, (2) calling cleanup() invokes all three successfully-registered unlisten fns, (3) console.warn was called (spy on console.warn) with a message about the failed subscription. This test FAILS against current code because Promise.all rejects entirely when any listener fails, discarding the resolved UnlistenFn values and never returning a cleanup function.

REVIEW FIX [resource_leak]: In engineStore.ts subscribeToEvents(), replace Promise.all with Promise.allSettled. After settlement, partition results: extract fulfilled .value entries as UnlistenFn[], extract rejected .reason entries as Error[]. For each rejected result, call console.warn('Failed to subscribe to event:', reason). Return a cleanup function that iterates only over the successfully-resolved UnlistenFn array and calls each one. This ensures: (a) partial failures don't discard successful subscriptions, (b) successful subscriptions are always cleanable, (c) failures are surfaced for debugging. All engine store tests pass including the new resource leak test from step-20.

REVIEW FIX FINAL VERIFICATION: Run full test suite (vitest run) and type-check (tsc --noEmit). Verify all tests pass and TypeScript compiles cleanly. Confirm all three review blocking issues are resolved: (1) applyValueUpdates/applyConstraintUpdates use batch() for single reactive flush, (2) subscribeToEvents uses Promise.allSettled with graceful partial failure handling, (3) setCursorPosition defaults column to 0 via nullish coalescing.

design_decisions.decision: Create minimal scaffold as prerequisite since Task 81 gui/ doesn't exist
design_decisions.rationale: Task 81 (scaffold) not completed: no gui/ directory on any branch. Prerequisites create the minimal scaffold needed. Full scaffold with Tauri cargo config, CSS, etc. can be layered on later.

design_decisions.decision: Vitest with jsdom for store tests
design_decisions.rationale: Standard for Vite/SolidJS projects. jsdom provides DOM APIs for SolidJS reactivity. Store tests use createRoot for reactive context isolation per test.
Tauri APIs only available inside Tauri webview. vi.mock() provides clean isolation for bridge tests.

design_decisions.decision: Record<string, T> for store collections, not Map
design_decisions.rationale: Solid's createStore Proxy works with plain objects/arrays, not Map/Set. Record enables fine-grained per-key reactivity.
Solid createStore Proxy cannot track Set mutations. Array with includes/filter is compatible and sufficient for expected file counts.

design_decisions.decision: Factory function pattern (createXStore) for all stores
design_decisions.rationale: Singleton stores share state across tests causing flakiness. Factories enable fresh instances per test. In the app, stores created once and shared via context.
Binary array data from Rust. Typed arrays match WebGL expectations and enable efficient Tauri IPC transfer.
Tauri convention uses kebab-case ('mesh-update'). Bridge translates to camelCase JS names (onMeshUpdate) while using correct strings internally.

design_decisions.decision: RawMeshData wire type + conversion in bridge layer
design_decisions.rationale: Tauri IPC serializes via JSON, which cannot carry typed arrays natively — arrays arrive as number[]. Conversion to Float32Array/Uint32Array happens once in the bridge (onMeshUpdate, getInitialState) so the store and renderer always see typed arrays. This keeps conversion cost O(n) per update, not per frame.

design_decisions.decision: Default column to 0 in setCursorPosition via nullish coalescing
design_decisions.rationale: column ?? 0 is the safest fix: it preserves the optional parameter API, guarantees the type contract { line: number; column: number } at runtime, and column 0 is a sensible default (start of line). Overloads would be heavier for the same result.

design_decisions.decision: Use batch() from solid-js for bulk setState loops in engine store
design_decisions.rationale: Review fix [missing_batch_for_reactive_updates]: Without batch(), each setState in a loop triggers a synchronous reactive flush. On the hot evaluation path (100+ parameter updates from the engine), this causes N independent re-render cycles. batch() coalesces all updates into a single reactive notification, matching the expected semantics for bulk operations.

design_decisions.decision: Use Promise.allSettled for event subscription with graceful partial failure
design_decisions.rationale: Review fix [resource_leak]: Promise.all discards all fulfilled results when any promise rejects, leaking active event subscriptions that can never be cleaned up. Promise.allSettled lets us collect successful UnlistenFn values and clean them up regardless of whether other subscriptions failed. Failures are logged via console.warn for debugging.

design_decisions.decision: Verify batch behavior via createEffect reactive notification counting
design_decisions.rationale: SolidJS effects run synchronously. Without batch, N setState calls trigger N effect re-runs. With batch, they trigger 1. A counter in createEffect that reads the tracked state (via JSON.stringify) provides a reliable observable for verifying batching correctness in tests.

reuse.where: crates/reify-types/src/geometry.rs (Mesh), value.rs (Value, DeterminacyState, Satisfaction), constraint.rs (ConstraintResult), diagnostics.rs (Diagnostic, SourceSpan)
reuse.how: TS IPC types mirror serialized forms: MeshData mirrors Mesh, ValueData mirrors ValueCellId+Value, Satisfaction maps to string union.
All stores use createStore([initial]) returning [state, setState]. Actions use setState with path-based updates.
Wraps multiple setState calls so they trigger a single reactive notification. Used in applyValueUpdates and applyConstraintUpdates for bulk update performance.
Bridge wraps invoke<T>(cmd, args) for commands, listen<T>(event, handler) for events. UnlistenFn for cleanup.

reuse.where: All reify-* crates follow test-first pattern
reuse.how: Same discipline for TypeScript: test files first, each step atomic. Factory pattern for test isolation mirrors Rust per-test setup.

Exported function converts RawMeshData (number[] from JSON wire) to MeshData (typed arrays). Used by bridge.ts in onMeshUpdate and getInitialState. Downstream consumers (engine store, renderer) never see number[] mesh data.
